title: Happy Place
author: Emily Henry
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Happy Place" book by Emily Henry?
Options:
A. I'll have more time to adapt to the residency, and then we can sort out everything else. Months go by, and his grief doesn't decrease. Mine hovers nearby, always ready to trip me up. Everything still makes me think of Hank, of what Gloria must be feeling, what Wyn must be internalizing. Something as innocent as a car ad can overwhelm me. I start taking long showers so I can release it all without piling my pain onto his. Wyn starts taking long runs to burn it all away. We don't paint the apartment. One weekend he offers, but between his two jobs, it's his one day off, and he looks so tired.
B. I will have more time to get accustomed to the residency, and then we can figure out everything else. Months pass, and his grief doesn't lessen. Mine lingers nearby, always ready to make me stumble. Everything still reminds me of Hank, of what Gloria must be feeling, what Wyn must be keeping inside. Something as harmless as a car commercial can overwhelm me. I start taking long showers so I can let it all out without adding my pain to his. Wyn starts taking long runs to work it all off. We don't paint the apartment. One weekend he offers, but between his two jobs, it's his one day off, and he looks exhausted.
C. I will have more time to get used to the residency, and then we can figure out everything else. Months pass, and his grief doesn't lessen. Mine lingers close by, always ready to make me stumble. Everything still reminds me of Hank, of what Gloria must be feeling, what Wyn must be keeping inside. Something as harmless as a car commercial can overwhelm me. I start taking long showers so I can let it all out without adding my pain to his. Wyn starts taking long runs to work it all off. We don't paint the apartment. One weekend he offers, but between his two jobs, it's his one day off, and he looks so exhausted.
D. Ill have more time to adjust to the residency, and then we can figure everything else out. Months go by, and his grief doesnt abate. Mine hovers close too, always waiting to trip me up. Everything still makes me think of Hank, of what Gloria must be feeling, what Wyn must be keeping inside. Something as innocent as a car commercial can split me open. I start taking long showers so I can let it all out without piling my pain onto his. Wyn starts taking long runs to burn it all off. We dont paint the apartment. One weekend he offers, but between his two jobs, its his one day off, and he looks so tired.
